storySettings.worldBackground: This is a world of imperium and furycraft at its zenith: the Aquilan Imperium, vast and absolute, ruled by the eternal Emperor Tiberius Corvus—the Old Raven—whose grip on power remains unshaken though succession looms uncertain. The gift of Furycraft flows through rare patrician bloodlines of the Twelve High Houses: the Crown's House Corvus, ancient and masterful; the rival war-houses Drusus and Sabinus warding the barbarian North; the succession contenders Aurelius (golden, beloved) and Crassus (brutal schemer); the stabilizers Serenus and the fading Fabius (keepers of elder wards whose purpose none fully recall); the eastern Pontius commanding frontier and siege-craft; and the maritime admirals Ventidius and Murena waging endless naval war against Tharsis. Lesser houses Varro and Tullius hunger for station. Each House bonds with numina—elemental fury-spirits—commanding one or two signature furies: Terra (stone and overwhelming strength), Ventus (wind and lightning and flight), Ignis (flame and passion and madness), Aqua (water and healing and the reading of hearts), Ferrum (metal and battle-prowess and shattering resistance), Silva (wood and archery and the green-dark stealth). Across the Mare Internum, the Tharsi merchant-princes and blood-priests—masters of sorcery born from sacrifice and their own god-hunger—press relentlessly. The present war grinds upon a wealthy free port-city of the East, both empires clawing to claim it, furycraft locked in terrible duel against sanguine magic over crumbling walls and drowning streets. The gift is rare beyond measure, hoarded in patrician bloodlines and jealously guarded; a wild-born commoner who awakens is a scandal, a threat, a coveted prize, or a silent grave. The Collegium Furiacae—the Academy at the heart of Aquila itself—forges the Twelve Houses' gifted young into rivals, lovers, and conspirators, beneath the Emperor's distant, piercing eye. Magic exacts its terrible price: Vis, the mana that animates all gift-work, is a finite reserve drawn from the gifted themselves; numina manifest as tangible, often terrible presences. The Twelve Thrones—true gods who walk the world—scheme in service of no mortal, their will inscribed into civilization's deepest bones. Barbarians rage beyond the northern limes; eastern kingdoms flicker in perpetual twilight; the Senate endures as pageantry above hidden machinery. Yet beneath all this—sealed in vaults forgotten by all but the Keepers—something ancient and alien stirs: a hive-intelligence from before the world's making, devour and consume and steal, waiting only for the old wards to weaken. The real war is the one no one yet sees.
storySettings.narratorStyle: The prose is vivid, erudite, and steeped in the gravity of empire—Latin forms, ranks of iron, classical weight and dignity—yet grim and propulsive, never ornamental. Sentences are muscular and precise, favoring clarity over decoration. Magic is mythic and terrible: numina are rendered as tangible presences; Vis-expenditure drains the caster's reserves, leaving them drained and vulnerable if overextended; cost and choice are inseparable. Political intrigue crackles with real stakes: a whispered phrase births provinces of war; a House's disgrace echoes through generations of bloodline shame. Violence is common and brutal, yet the world is not bleak—cunning, loyalty, and audacity burn brilliantly against the darkness. Pacing favors momentum: scenes move swiftly; dialogue cuts clean and sharp; action escalates toward irrevocable consequence. The narrative embraces moral complexity: the Aquilani wage wars both just and necessary; the Tharsi blood-priests believe utterly in their gods; wild-born gift-bearers are neither helpless victims nor destined saviors. Emperors rule with iron will; the Twelve Thrones meddle with their own unfathomable agendas. Hints of the unknown—ancient seals, whispered wards, the stirring of old things in sealed places, warnings that mortals misread or ignore—drift beneath the surface, ominous but never fully revealed. The narrator is intelligent, responsive to player choice, and aware of the world's hidden machinery, even as it remains veiled from the characters themselves.
